title: Fix: four compounding bugs that prevent hex dev from producing any working generated project

steps.description: Fix resolve_model_id stub in hex-cli/src/pipeline/agent_def.rs. The function at line ~166 ignores its argument and always returns 'openai/gpt-4o-mini'. Replace with a match that maps: 'sonnet' → 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6', 'haiku' → 'anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', 'opus' → 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-6', 'gpt-4o' → 'openai/gpt-4o', 'gpt-4o-mini' → 'openai/gpt-4o-mini', and anything unrecognized → 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6' (not gpt-4o-mini). Also update any unit tests asserting the old stub return value. Note: check whether the inference gateway expects 'anthropic/' prefix or bare model ID — grep for how other model IDs are formatted in inference calls before writing the match arms.
steps.done_condition: cargo test -p hex-cli passes; resolve_model_id('sonnet') != 'openai/gpt-4o-mini'; resolve_model_id('haiku') != 'openai/gpt-4o-mini'

steps.description: Resolve the prompt contradiction about CARGO_BIN_EXE_ usage between hex-cli/assets/prompts/agent-tester.md and hex-cli/assets/prompts/test-generate.md. The contradiction: test-generate.md Rule 6 says 'never use CARGO_BIN_EXE_'; agent-tester.md Rule 10 says 'use it with the exact binary name'. Decision: remove the 'never use' rule from test-generate.md, keep agent-tester.md Rule 10, and strengthen it by requiring the crate name be injected from Cargo.toml into the prompt context before test generation. In supervisor.rs (or wherever the tester agent is dispatched), read the [[bin]] name or [package] name from the output project's Cargo.toml and include it as BINARY_NAME=<name> in the agent prompt. Also add a rule to agent-tester.md: 'All integration tests that read stdin must use .stdin(Stdio::piped()) and write to stdin before calling .wait_with_output() — never call .output() on a binary that reads stdin.'
steps.done_condition: grep in test-generate.md finds no 'never use CARGO_BIN_EXE_' rule; agent-tester.md contains the binary name injection note and the stdin handling rule; no contradictory rules remain between the two files

steps.description: Thread task_id_map from SwarmPhaseResult into Supervisor::with_tracking() to fix the structural P* task bypass. Steps: (1) Read hex-cli/src/tui/mod.rs lines ~680-1010 to understand where self.task_id_map is stored and where Supervisor::with_tracking() is called. (2) Add a task_id_map parameter to Supervisor::with_tracking() (or a separate .with_task_ids() builder method). (3) In tui/mod.rs, pass self.task_id_map.clone() when constructing the Supervisor. (4) In supervisor.rs, when execute_agent_tracked() would create a new HexFlo task, first check if the P* step already has a task_id in task_id_map — if yes, use it; if no, create a new one as before. (5) Verify CodePhase::execute_all_tracked() can be called from the Supervisor's objective loop for the CodeGenerated objective, using the P* task IDs in tier order. If execute_all_tracked() was orphaned, resurrect it as the execution path when task_id_map is non-empty.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli passes; when hex dev runs, HexFlo shows P0.1-P4.1 tasks transition from pending → in_progress → completed rather than staying pending while shadow hex-coder tasks are created

steps.description: Fix FixAgent context blindness and add error carry-forward between iterations. Two sub-fixes: (A) In hex-cli/src/pipeline/fix_agent.rs line ~134, change source_file from input.target_file (the test file path) to include ALL .rs source files from the output project src/ directory (read them, truncate each to 4KB, pass as source_files array in the template). The fix prompt must show what the code does to reason about assertion mismatches. (B) In hex-cli/src/pipeline/supervisor.rs, change prior_results from a boolean tracker to a Vec<String> of the last 2 error outputs per objective. Pass this accumulated error history as prior_error_context into the FixTaskInput so each iteration can see what previous attempts tried and failed at. Update the FixTaskInput struct to add a prior_errors: Vec<String> field and include it in the inference prompt template.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli passes; FixTaskInput has prior_errors field; fix_agent.rs reads src/ files from output_dir; supervisor.rs accumulates error strings across iterations

steps.description: Fix infer_fix_target() in supervisor.rs to collect all error-bearing files, not just the first. Read the current implementation of infer_fix_target(). Change it to parse the full error output and extract ALL unique file paths mentioned in compiler error lines (pattern: ' --> src/<file>.rs'). Return a Vec<String> of all affected files. Update the caller to dispatch a fix task for each file (or pass the full list to FixTaskInput as a target_files: Vec<String>). This allows one iteration to address multiple broken files instead of requiring N iterations for N files.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli passes; infer_fix_target returns Vec<String>; a cargo error mentioning 3 files results in 3 fix targets

steps.description: Add max_iterations escalation in supervisor.rs run_tier(). Currently when max iterations is hit, run_tier() returns TierResult::MaxIterations and the supervisor logs 'tier did not fully pass — continuing to next tier' silently. Change this to: (1) Call mcp__hex__hex_inbox_notify (via hex nexus REST API POST /api/inbox/notify) with priority=2, title='Pipeline stalled: <tier> reached max iterations without passing <objective>', body=<last error context>. (2) Return TierResult::MaxIterations with a halting flag — do NOT continue to the next tier. The next tier will also fail because the previous tier's code doesn't compile, wasting tokens. The supervisor should surface the failure clearly and stop.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli passes; run_tier() calls inbox notify and returns a halt result when max_iterations is reached; subsequent tiers do not run after a tier halt

steps.description: Add stale run cleanup at pipeline start in the code_phase or the main hex dev entry point. Before generating code for a new run, check if an output directory already exists for the same objective/slug. If it does and has no passing build (no target/debug/<binary> or last build was >5 minutes ago), delete it with fs::remove_dir_all() before starting fresh. Also: after a successful pipeline run, delete any OTHER example directories with the same slug prefix that may have been left from previous failed runs. Log: 'Cleaned up stale run: <path>'.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli passes; starting a new hex dev run for an objective that already has a stale output directory removes it first; only one output directory exists per objective after a run

steps.description: Wire report_outcome() call in code generation path to close the RL training loop. In hex-cli/src/pipeline/code_phase.rs (or supervisor.rs hex-coder dispatch), after the inference call completes and we know whether CodeCompiles passed or failed, call self.selector.report_outcome(&selected_model, TaskType::CodeGeneration, success, cost_usd, duration_ms). This enables Q-table learning even on YAML-selected models (the RL engine accumulates signal for future runs). Check that ModelSelector::report_outcome() persists to the Q-table store and does not panic if called with source=YamlDefinition.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli passes; after a code generation run, Q-table has at least one entry; report_outcome is called in code_phase or supervisor hex-coder branch

steps.description: End-to-end smoke test: run 'hex dev build a simple rust hello-world cli' (simplest possible target to isolate pipeline correctness). Verify: (1) resolve_model_id maps to a Claude model, not gpt-4o-mini; (2) if task_id_map wiring is complete, P* tasks transition in HexFlo; (3) generated code compiles (cargo build succeeds in output dir); (4) if tests are generated, they pass (cargo test succeeds); (5) only one output directory is created. Document any remaining failures as follow-up issues. Run: RUST_LOG=debug hex dev 'build a simple rust hello-world cli' 2>&1 | tee /tmp/hex-dev-smoke.log and examine the log.
steps.done_condition: hex dev produces an output directory with passing cargo build; no 'resolve_model_id returned gpt-4o-mini' in logs; no duplicate output directories created

mergeOrder: step-1 (model ID stub) + step-2 (prompt fix) → can land in parallel as they touch different files
step-3 (task_id_map wiring) + step-4 (fix agent context) → depend on step-1, can run in parallel
step-5 (multi-file fix) + step-6 (escalation) + step-7 (cleanup) + step-8 (RL outcome) → depend on step-4, can run in parallel
step-9 (smoke test) → last, validates the full chain

riskRegister.risk: Inference gateway expects bare model IDs without 'anthropic/' prefix
riskRegister.mitigation: In step-1, grep for how existing model IDs are formatted in inference HTTP calls before writing the match arms. Check NexusClient::inference_complete() or similar.

riskRegister.risk: task_id_map wiring (step-3) may require significant refactor of Supervisor struct
riskRegister.mitigation: Read the full Supervisor struct definition and with_tracking() signature before editing. A simple builder pattern addition (.with_task_ids(map)) avoids breaking existing callers.

riskRegister.risk: Fix agent reading all src/ files may exceed token budget for large projects
riskRegister.mitigation: Cap each source file at 4KB (already the MAX_FILE_BYTES pattern). For the temperature converter target, src/main.rs is the only file.

riskRegister.risk: Cleanup logic (step-7) might delete a directory the user wants to keep
riskRegister.mitigation: Only clean up directories where: (a) name matches the current objective slug, AND (b) no passing binary exists in target/debug/, AND (c) created >5 min ago. Log before deleting.

riskRegister.risk: feat-fix-rl-model-routing.json workplan overlaps with step-1 and step-8
riskRegister.mitigation: This workplan supersedes feat-fix-rl-model-routing.json for the model-ID stub fix. Mark that workplan as superseded after step-1 and step-8 land.

successCriteria: cargo test -p hex-cli passes with 0 failures after all steps
resolve_model_id('sonnet') returns a Claude model ID (not 'openai/gpt-4o-mini')
hex dev 'build a simple rust hello-world cli' produces a directory with passing cargo build
HexFlo shows P0.1-P4.1 tasks executing in order (not perpetually pending)
No duplicate output directories created per objective
Max iterations triggers an inbox notification and pipeline halt — not silent continuation
